title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLVIII. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman,
            And she sold puddings and pies;
        She went to the mill,
            And the dust flew in her eyes:
        Hot pies and cold pies to sell!
            Wherever she goes, - 
        You may follow her by the smell.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCLVIII. Gaffers And Gammers." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
